methods.description: Process for sampling sediment from cylinders at various stages of the experiment to analyze different components such as stable isotopes, bacterial cell numbers, and PLFA.

steps.description: We sampled sediment on the following days: 0, 29, 31, 74 and 77.
steps.searchable_summary: schedule sediment sampling on specified days

steps.description: On each sampling day, a sterile section of pipette was inserted into the sediment of each cylinder to extract a single sample of the entire sediment depth (0-10 cm).
steps.searchable_summary: extract sediment sample using pipette

steps.description: The slurry got suspended with the pore water resulting in approximately 10 ml of sediment, which was extracted from each cylinder and divided for subsequent analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: suspend sediment in pore water and divide for analysis

steps.description: The samples used for DNA and RNA co-extraction were placed in a 2 ml tube containing 0.5 g of 1 mm zirconia-silica beads and were immediately centrifuged to remove excess water.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for DNA/RNA extraction and centrifuge

steps.description: The sediment subsamples for bacterial cell number and biomass analysis were preserved by adding 4 ml of 4% formaldehyde and stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve sediment samples with formaldehyde

steps.description: The samples for PLFA analysis were freeze dried and stored at −20°C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze dry samples for PLFA analysis

methods.description: Set-up of microcosms to simulate different hydrological regimes and track carbon input and fate in the system.

steps.description: The sediment was thoroughly mixed and equally distributed into 15 cylinders, resulting in a 10 cm layer of sediment within the microcosm.
steps.searchable_summary: distribute mixed sediment into cylinders

steps.description: Each cylinder was filled with 12 cm of filtered lake water and had a headspace of 10 cm above the water.
steps.searchable_summary: fill cylinders with lake water and create headspace

steps.description: To simulate the input of allochthonous carbon, 300 mg of beech leaves was added to each cylinder.
steps.searchable_summary: add beech leaves to cylinders

steps.description: The water column above the sediment was continuously oxygenated using aquarium pumps.
steps.searchable_summary: oxygenate water column with pumps

steps.description: The 15 microcosm cylinders were exposed to varying hydrological regimes in three different treatments: 'wet system', 'dry system', and 'rewet system'.
steps.searchable_summary: apply hydrological treatments to microcosms

steps.description: The periodic water table changes within the cylinders in the REWET system were achieved by moving the cylinders in and out of a water reservoir.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate water table changes in REWET system

methods.description: Procedure for measuring CO2 concentrations and isotopic composition in the microcosms to assess respiration rates and carbon source utilization.

steps.description: Gas measurements of CO2 and its carbon isotopic composition were collected by first sealing the cylinders gas-tight allowing for CO2 concentrations to increase over 18 h.
steps.searchable_summary: seal cylinders and collect CO2 gas measurements

steps.description: 12 ml of gas from the headspace of each cylinder was transferred into an N2-flushed 12 ml Exetainer.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer gas sample to Exetainer

steps.description: For the δ13CO analysis, we used a Thermo Delta V Advantage isotope ratio mass spectrometer interfaced to a GasBench-II automated headspace sampler.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ13CO using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: Respiration rates were calculated from the linear increase in CO2 concentration over time in the closed system.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate respiration rates from CO2 concentration

methods.description: Procedure for extracting and analyzing phospholipid fatty acids (PLFA) from sediment samples to determine microbial community structure and carbon isotopic composition.

steps.description: For PLFA extraction, 2 g of freeze-dried sediment sample was extracted with a modified one-phase Bligh/Dyer method.
steps.searchable_summary: extract PLFA using Bligh/Dyer method

steps.description: The lipids were separated into neutral, glyco-and polar lipids using solid phase extraction with silicic acid columns.
steps.searchable_summary: separate lipids using solid phase extraction

steps.description: The polar fraction containing the phospholipids was collected, and the fatty acid 19:0 was added as an internal standard.
steps.searchable_summary: collect polar lipids and add internal standard

steps.description: The samples were subjected to a mild alkaline methanolysis for conversion to fatty acid methyl esters (FAME) for analysis by gas chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: convert lipids to FAME for GC analysis

steps.description: Quantification and identification of FAMEs and the stable carbon isotopic composition of the individual PLFAs were determined on a Thermo GC/C-IRMS system.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze FAMEs and isotopic composition using GC/C-IRMS
